title: Fix backend canister ID wiring — resolve 'Backend Connection Failed' error

requirements.text: Audit the frontend canister actor initialization code (in useActor.ts and any related config files such as canister_ids.json, dfx.json, or environment variable resolution) and fix any mismatch between the deployed backend canister ID and the ID the frontend is using to create the actor, so that the 'Backend actor not available. Please check your connection.' error is resolved and the app connects successfully to the DroneWash Motoko canister.

quotes: Backend actor not available. Please check your connection.

acceptanceCriteria: The 'Backend Connection Failed' screen no longer appears after login
The frontend successfully initializes the backend actor and loads dashboard data
The canister ID used in the frontend actor initialization matches the deployed Motoko canister ID
Clicking 'Retry Connection' (if still present) successfully connects without a page reload

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts or frontend/src/main.tsx
Do not change backend Motoko code — this is a frontend configuration fix only
nonGoals: Modifying backend data models or Motoko actor logic
